company: Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing Ltd
one_line_thesis: Asia flagship exchange croupier at cycle-peak earnings; ~-0.15% base annual return at HK$383 implies watch until turnover normalizes or price resets.

deep_dive.executive_summary: HKEX is Asia's flagship exchange and clearing croupier with 74% EBITDA margins and a durable regulatory moat around Hong Kong market infrastructure. FY2025 EPS reached HK$14.05 with full-year dividend HK$12.52 per share (`0388.HK/investor-documents/ir-0388.hk/260316sr_e.pdf`). We normalize owner earnings to HK$11 per share, below spot EPS, to adjust for cyclical turnover and margin-fund pass-through. At HK$383 (2026-06-10 close), total synthesis implies about -0.15% per year over seven years, far below our 15% hold band. Proof-backed component base value HK$258.45 per share. Lawrence base s...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_claim.description: One HKEX ordinary share, including cash-equity and derivatives croupier fees, Connect/data reinvestment, net financial claims after clearing pass-through, and peak-cycle reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating engine and reinvestment valued separately; clearing member deposits and margin-fund pass-through excluded from net financial claim.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Cash equities, derivatives, clearing, and LME fee infrastructure

valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key core_engine; no other component capitalizes the same claim.

total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

dissents.falsifier: FCF yield < 0.5% or commoditized archetype (turnaround/optionality only)
dissents.label: Chris Hohn / TCI

dissents.falsifier: Asset value lacks an independently evidenced realization path or is already capitalized in operating cash flow.
dissents.label: Klarman / Baupost

timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue HK$8.2B (+20% vs Q1 2025) confirms strong run-rate after FY2025 record
FY2025 results: EPS HK$14.05 and full-year dividend HK$12.52 per share (+10% YoY)
FY2024 record year: revenue HK$22.4B (+9%), profit HK$13.1B (+10%), ADT HK$131.8B (+26%)
Bond Connect southbound trading launched, extending fixed-income access channel
Nicolas Aguzin appointed Group CEO, succeeding Charles Li after long tenure
Shenzhen Stock Connect opened, deepening mainland-Hong Kong equity link
Stock Connect launch with Shanghai expanded cross-border equity flow through Hong Kong
Completed acquisition of London Metal Exchange, adding global commodities clearing to franchise

industry.structure: HKEX is the dominant listing and trading gateway between international capital and Chinese issuers, operating cash equities, derivatives, LME commodities, and central clearing with high consolidated margins (~74% EBITDA).
industry.share_shift: Mainland exchanges (Shanghai, Shenzhen) grow domestic listings, but Connect programs keep Hong Kong as the preferred offshore channel for China exposure; global exchange peers (CME, ICE, LSEG) compete on derivatives and data.
industry.trend: Record FY2024–FY2025 volumes reflect cyclical boom in China-linked trading; regulatory scrutiny of Hong Kong's financial-center role and LME market-structure reforms remain ongoing watch items.

top_claims.claim: Hong Kong restaurants score extra HK$500 million thanks to World Cup    South China Morning Post

HKEX is Asia's flagship exchange and clearing croupier with 74% EBITDA margins and a durable regulatory moat around Hong Kong market infrastructure. FY2024 was a record year: revenue HK$22.4 billion, profit HK$13.1 billion, EPS HK$10.32; FY2025 EPS reached HK$14.05 with full-year dividend HK$12.52 per share (HKEX Group
We expect **-0.15%** per year over 7 years at **~$383.0** on **$11.0/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: Prolonged slump in cash-equity turnover and China-linked flow after the FY2024–FY2025 boom, compressing trading fees and clearing balances.
